verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचजनेषु प्रगृणत्स्वेवं पृथुं पृथुलविक्रमम् ।तत्रोपजग्मुर्मुनयश्चत्वार: सूर्यवर्चस: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācajaneṣu pragṛṇatsv evaṁpṛthuṁ pṛthula-vikramamtatropajagmur munayaścatvāraḥ sūrya-varcasaḥ
verses.text: The great sage Maitreya said: While the citizens were thus praying to the most powerful King Pṛthu, the four Kumāras, who were as bright as the sun, arrived on the spot.

verses.sanskrit: तांस्तु सिद्धेश्वरान् राजा व्योम्नोऽवतरतोऽर्चिषा ।लोकानपापान् कुर्वाणान् सानुगोऽचष्ट लक्षितान् ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāṁs tu siddheśvarān rājāvyomno ’vatarato ’rciṣālokān apāpān kurvāṇānsānugo ’caṣṭa lakṣitān
verses.text: Seeing the glowing effulgence of the four Kumāras, the masters of all mystic power, the King and his associates could recognize them as they descended from the sky.

verses.sanskrit: तद्दर्शनोद्गतान् प्राणान् प्रत्यादित्सुरिवोत्थित: ।ससदस्यानुगो वैन्य इन्द्रियेशो गुणानिव ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tad-darśanodgatān prāṇānpratyāditsur ivotthitaḥsa-sadasyānugo vainyaindriyeśo guṇān iva
verses.text: Seeing the four Kumāras, Pṛthu Mahārāja was greatly anxious to receive them. Therefore the King, with all his officers, very hastily got up, as anxiously as a conditioned soul whose senses are immediately attracted by the modes of material nature.

verses.sanskrit: गौरवाद्यन्त्रित: सभ्य: प्रश्रयानतकन्धर: ।विधिवत्पूजयां चक्रे गृहीताध्यर्हणासनान् ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: gauravād yantritaḥ sabhyaḥpraśrayānata-kandharaḥvidhivat pūjayāṁ cakregṛhītādhyarhaṇāsanān
verses.text: When the great sages accepted their reception, according to the instructions of the śāstras, and finally took their seats offered by the King, the King, influenced by the glories of the sages, immediately bowed down. Thus he worshiped the four Kumāras.

verses.sanskrit: तत्पादशौचसलिलैर्मार्जितालकबन्धन: ।तत्र शीलवतां वृत्तमाचरन्मानयन्निव ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tat-pāda-śauca-salilairmārjitālaka-bandhanaḥtatra śīlavatāṁ vṛttamācaran mānayann iva
verses.text: After this, the King took the water which had washed the lotus feet of the Kumāras and sprinkled it over his hair. By such respectful actions, the King, as an exemplary personality, showed how to receive a spiritually advanced personality.

verses.sanskrit: हाटकासन आसीनान् स्वधिष्ण्येष्विव पावकान् ।श्रद्धासंयमसंयुक्त: प्रीत: प्राह भवाग्रजान् ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: hāṭakāsana āsīnānsva-dhiṣṇyeṣv iva pāvakānśraddhā-saṁyama-saṁyuktaḥprītaḥ prāha bhavāgrajān
verses.text: The four great sages were elder to Lord Śiva, and when they were seated on the golden throne, they appeared just like fire blazing on an altar. Mahārāja Pṛthu, out of his great gentleness and respect for them, began to speak with great restraint as follows.

verses.sanskrit: पृथुरुवाचअहो आचरितं किं मे मङ्गलं मङ्गलायना: ।यस्य वो दर्शनं ह्यासीद्दुर्दर्शानां च योगिभि: ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: pṛthur uvācaaho ācaritaṁ kiṁ memaṅgalaṁ maṅgalāyanāḥyasya vo darśanaṁ hy āsīddurdarśānāṁ ca yogibhiḥ
verses.text: King Pṛthu spoke: My dear great sages, auspiciousness personified, it is very difficult for even the mystic yogīs to see you. Indeed, you are very rarely seen. I do not know what kind of pious activity I performed for you to grace me by appearing before me without difficulty.

verses.sanskrit: किं तस्य दुर्लभतरमिह लोके परत्र च ।यस्य विप्रा: प्रसीदन्ति शिवो विष्णुश्च सानुग: ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: kiṁ tasya durlabhataramiha loke paratra cayasya viprāḥ prasīdantiśivo viṣṇuś ca sānugaḥ
verses.text: Any person upon whom the brāhmaṇas and Vaiṣṇavas are pleased can achieve anything which is very rare to obtain in this world as well as after death. Not only that, but one also receives the favor of the auspicious Lord Śiva and Lord Viṣṇu, who accompany the brāhmaṇas and Vaiṣṇavas.

verses.sanskrit: नैव लक्षयते लोको लोकान् पर्यटतोऽपि यान् ।यथा सर्वद‍ृशं सर्व आत्मानं येऽस्य हेतव: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: naiva lakṣayate lokolokān paryaṭato ’pi yānyathā sarva-dṛśaṁ sarvaātmānaṁ ye ’sya hetavaḥ
verses.text: Pṛthu Mahārāja continued: Although you are traveling in all planetary systems, people cannot know you, just as they cannot know the Supersoul, although He is within everyone’s heart as the witness of everything. Even Lord Brahmā and Lord Śiva cannot understand the Supersoul.

verses.sanskrit: अधना अपि ते धन्या: साधवो गृहमेधिन: ।यद्गृहा ह्यर्हवर्याम्बुतृणभूमीश्वरावरा: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: adhanā api te dhanyāḥsādhavo gṛha-medhinaḥyad-gṛhā hy arha-varyāmbu-tṛṇa-bhūmīśvarāvarāḥ
verses.text: A person who is not very rich and is attached to family life becomes highly glorified when saintly persons are present in his home. The master and servants who are engaged in offering the exalted visitors water, a sitting place and paraphernalia for reception are glorified, and the home itself is also glorified.

verses.sanskrit: व्यालालयद्रुमा वै तेष्वरिक्ताखिलसम्पद: ।यद्गृहास्तीर्थपादीयपादतीर्थविवर्जिता: ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: vyālālaya-drumā vai teṣvariktākhila-sampadaḥyad-gṛhās tīrtha-pādīya-pādatīrtha-vivarjitāḥ
verses.text: On the contrary, even though full of all opulence and material prosperity, any householder’s house where the devotees of the Lord are never allowed to come in, and where there is no water for washing their feet, is to be considered a tree in which all venomous serpents live.

verses.sanskrit: स्वागतं वो द्विजश्रेष्ठा यद्‌व्रतानि मुमुक्षव: ।चरन्ति श्रद्धया धीरा बाला एव बृहन्ति च ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: svāgataṁ vo dvija-śreṣṭhāyad-vratāni mumukṣavaḥcaranti śraddhayā dhīrābālā eva bṛhanti ca
verses.text: Mahārāja Pṛthu offered his welcome to the four Kumāras, addressing them as the best of the brāhmaṇas. He welcomed them, saying: From the beginning of your birth you strictly observed the vows of celibacy, and although you are experienced in the path of liberation, you are keeping yourselves just like small children.

verses.sanskrit: कच्चिन्न: कुशलं नाथा इन्द्रियार्थार्थवेदिनाम् ।व्यसनावाप एतस्मिन्पतितानां स्वकर्मभि: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: kaccin naḥ kuśalaṁ nāthāindriyārthārtha-vedināmvyasanāvāpa etasminpatitānāṁ sva-karmabhiḥ
verses.text: Pṛthu Mahārāja inquired from the sages about persons entangled in this dangerous material existence because of their previous actions. Could such persons, whose only aim is sense gratification, be blessed with any good fortune?

verses.sanskrit: भवत्सु कुशलप्रश्न आत्मारामेषु नेष्यते ।कुशलाकुशला यत्र न सन्ति मतिवृत्तय: ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhavatsu kuśala-praśnaātmārāmeṣu neṣyatekuśalākuśalā yatrana santi mati-vṛttayaḥ
verses.text: Pṛthu Mahārāja continued: My dear sirs, there is no need to ask about your good and bad fortune because you are always absorbed in spiritual bliss. The mental concoction of the auspicious and inauspicious does not exist in you.

verses.sanskrit: तदहं कृतविश्रम्भ: सुहृदो वस्तपस्विनाम् ।सम्पृच्छे भव एतस्मिन् क्षेम: केनाञ्जसा भवेत् ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tad ahaṁ kṛta-viśrambhaḥsuhṛdo vas tapasvināmsampṛcche bhava etasminkṣemaḥ kenāñjasā bhavet
verses.text: I am completely assured that personalities like you are the only friends for persons who are blazing in the fire of material existence. I therefore ask you how in this material world we can very soon achieve the ultimate goal of life.

verses.sanskrit: व्यक्तमात्मवतामात्मा भगवानात्मभावन: ।स्वानामनुग्रहायेमां सिद्धरूपी चरत्यज: ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: vyaktam ātmavatām ātmābhagavān ātma-bhāvanaḥsvānām anugrahāyemāṁsiddha-rūpī caraty ajaḥ
verses.text: The Supreme Personality of Godhead is always anxious to elevate the living entities, who are His parts and parcels, and for their special benefit the Lord travels all over the world in the form of self-realized persons like you.

verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचपृथोस्तत्सूक्तमाकर्ण्य सारं सुष्ठु मितं मधु ।स्मयमान इव प्रीत्या कुमार: प्रत्युवाच ह ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācapṛthos tat sūktam ākarṇyasāraṁ suṣṭhu mitaṁ madhusmayamāna iva prītyākumāraḥ pratyuvāca ha
verses.text: The great sage Maitreya continued: Thus Sanat-kumāra, the best of the celibates, after hearing the speech of Pṛthu Mahārāja, which was meaningful, appropriate, full of precise words and very sweet to hear, smiled with full satisfaction and began to speak as follows.

verses.sanskrit: सनत्कुमार उवाचसाधु पृष्टं महाराज सर्वभूतहितात्मना ।भवता विदुषा चापि साधूनां मतिरीद‍ृशी ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: sanat-kumāra uvācasādhu pṛṣṭaṁ mahārājasarva-bhūta-hitātmanābhavatā viduṣā cāpisādhūnāṁ matir īdṛśī
verses.text: Sanat-kumāra said: My dear King Pṛthu, I am very nicely questioned by you. Such questions are beneficial for all living entities, especially because they are raised by you, who are always thinking of the good of others. Although you know everything, you ask such questions because that is the behavior of saintly persons. Such intelligence is befitting your position.

verses.sanskrit: सङ्गम: खलु साधूनामुभयेषां च सम्मत: ।यत्सम्भाषणसम्प्रश्न: सर्वेषां वितनोति शम् ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: saṅgamaḥ khalu sādhūnāmubhayeṣāṁ ca sammataḥyat-sambhāṣaṇa-sampraśnaḥsarveṣāṁ vitanoti śam
verses.text: When there is a congregation of devotees, their discussions, questions and answers become conclusive to both the speaker and the audience. Thus such a meeting is beneficial for everyone’s real happiness.

verses.sanskrit: अस्त्येव राजन् भवतो मधुद्विष:पादारविन्दस्य गुणानुवादने ।रतिर्दुरापा विधुनोति नैष्ठिकीकामं कषायं मलमन्तरात्मन: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: asty eva rājan bhavato madhudviṣaḥpādāravindasya guṇānuvādaneratir durāpā vidhunoti naiṣṭhikīkāmaṁ kaṣāyaṁ malam antar-ātmanaḥ
verses.text: Sanat-kumāra continued: My dear King, you already have an inclination to glorify the lotus feet of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Such attachment is very difficult to achieve, but when one has attained such unflinching faith in the Lord, it automatically cleanses lusty desires from the core of the heart.

verses.sanskrit: शास्त्रेष्वियानेव सुनिश्चितो नृणांक्षेमस्य सध्र्‌यग्विमृशेषु हेतु: ।असङ्ग आत्मव्यतिरिक्त आत्मनिद‍ृढा रतिर्ब्रह्मणि निर्गुणे च या ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: śāstreṣv iyān eva suniścito nṛṇāṁkṣemasya sadhryag-vimṛśeṣu hetuḥasaṅga ātma-vyatirikta ātmanidṛḍhā ratir brahmaṇi nirguṇe ca yā
verses.text: It has been conclusively decided in the scriptures, after due consideration, that the ultimate goal for the welfare of human society is detachment from the bodily concept of life and increased and steadfast attachment for the Supreme Lord, who is transcendental, beyond the modes of material nature.

verses.sanskrit: सा श्रद्धया भगवद्धर्मचर्ययाजिज्ञासयाध्यात्मिकयोगनिष्ठया ।योगेश्वरोपासनया च नित्यंपुण्यश्रव:कथया पुण्यया च ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: sā śraddhayā bhagavad-dharma-caryayājijñāsayādhyātmika-yoga-niṣṭhayāyogeśvaropāsanayā ca nityaṁpuṇya-śravaḥ-kathayā puṇyayā ca
verses.text: Attachment for the Supreme can be increased by practicing devotional service, inquiring about the Supreme Personality of Godhead, applying bhakti-yoga in life, worshiping the Yogeśvara, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and by hearing and chanting about the glories of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. These actions are pious in themselves.

verses.sanskrit: अर्थेन्द्रियारामसगोष्ठ्यतृष्णयातत्सम्मतानामपरिग्रहेण च ।विविक्तरुच्या परितोष आत्मनिविना हरेर्गुणपीयूषपानात् ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: arthendriyārāma-sagoṣṭhy-atṛṣṇayātat-sammatānām aparigraheṇa cavivikta-rucyā paritoṣa ātmanivinā harer guṇa-pīyūṣa-pānāt
verses.text: One has to make progress in spiritual life by not associating with persons who are simply interested in sense gratification and making money. Not only such persons, but one who associates with such persons should be avoided. One should mold his life in such a way that he cannot live in peace without drinking the nectar of the glorification of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Hari. One can be thus elevated by being disgusted with the taste for sense enjoyment.

verses.sanskrit: अहिंसया पारमहंस्यचर्ययास्मृत्या मुकुन्दाचरिताग्र्यसीधुना ।यमैरकामैर्नियमैश्चाप्यनिन्दयानिरीहया द्वन्द्वतितिक्षया च ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: ahiṁsayā pāramahaṁsya-caryayāsmṛtyā mukundācaritāgrya-sīdhunāyamair akāmair niyamaiś cāpy anindayānirīhayā dvandva-titikṣayā ca
verses.text: A candidate for spiritual advancement must be nonviolent, must follow in the footsteps of great ācāryas, must always remember the nectar of the pastimes of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, must follow the regulative principles without material desire and, while following the regulative principles, should not blaspheme others. A devotee should lead a very simple life and not be disturbed by the duality of opposing elements. He should learn to tolerate them.

verses.sanskrit: हरेर्मुहुस्तत्परकर्णपूरगुणाभिधानेन विजृम्भमाणया ।भक्त्या ह्यसङ्ग: सदसत्यनात्मनिस्यान्निर्गुणे ब्रह्मणि चाञ्जसा रति: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: harer muhus tatpara-karṇa-pūra-guṇābhidhānena vijṛmbhamāṇayābhaktyā hy asaṅgaḥ sad-asaty anātmanisyān nirguṇe brahmaṇi cāñjasā ratiḥ
verses.text: The devotee should gradually increase the culture of devotional service by constant hearing of the transcendental qualities of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. These pastimes are like ornamental decorations on the ears of devotees. By rendering devotional service and transcending the material qualities, one can easily be fixed in transcendence in the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

verses.sanskrit: यदा रतिर्ब्रह्मणि नैष्ठिकी पुमा-नाचार्यवान् ज्ञानविरागरंहसा ।दहत्यवीर्यं हृदयं जीवकोशंपञ्चात्मकं योनिमिवोत्थितोऽग्नि: ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: yadā ratir brahmaṇi naiṣṭhikī pumānācāryavān jñāna-virāga-raṁhasādahaty avīryaṁ hṛdayaṁ jīva-kośaṁpañcātmakaṁ yonim ivotthito ’gniḥ
verses.text: Upon becoming fixed in his attachment to the Supreme Personality of Godhead by the grace of the spiritual master and by awakening knowledge and detachment, the living entity, situated within the heart of the body and covered by the five elements, burns up his material surroundings exactly as fire, arising from wood, burns the wood itself.

verses.sanskrit: दग्धाशयो मुक्तसमस्ततद्गुणोनैवात्मनो बहिरन्तर्विचष्टे ।परात्मनोर्यद्वय‍वधानं पुरस्तात्स्वप्ने यथा पुरुषस्तद्विनाशे ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: dagdhāśayo mukta-samasta-tad-guṇonaivātmano bahir antar vicaṣṭeparātmanor yad-vyavadhānaṁ purastātsvapne yathā puruṣas tad-vināśe
verses.text: When a person becomes devoid of all material desires and liberated from all material qualities, he transcends distinctions between actions executed externally and internally. At that time the difference between the soul and the Supersoul, which was existing before self-realization, is annihilated. When a dream is over, there is no longer a distinction between the dream and the dreamer.

verses.sanskrit: आत्मानमिन्द्रियार्थं च परं यदुभयोरपि ।सत्याशय उपाधौ वै पुमान् पश्यति नान्यदा ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: ātmānam indriyārthaṁ caparaṁ yad ubhayor apisaty āśaya upādhau vaipumān paśyati nānyadā
verses.text: When the soul exists for sense gratification, he creates different desires, and for that reason he becomes subjected to designations. But when one is in the transcendental position, he is no longer interested in anything except fulfilling the desires of the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: निमित्ते सति सर्वत्र जलादावपि पूरुष: ।आत्मनश्च परस्यापि भिदां पश्यति नान्यदा ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: nimitte sati sarvatrajalādāv api pūruṣaḥātmanaś ca parasyāpibhidāṁ paśyati nānyadā
verses.text: Only because of different causes does a person see a difference between himself and others, just as one sees the reflection of a body appearing differently manifested on water, on oil or in a mirror.

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रियैर्विषयाकृष्टैराक्षिप्तं ध्यायतां मन: ।चेतनां हरते बुद्धे: स्तम्बस्तोयमिव ह्रदात् ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: indriyair viṣayākṛṣṭairākṣiptaṁ dhyāyatāṁ manaḥcetanāṁ harate buddheḥstambas toyam iva hradāt
verses.text: When one’s mind and senses are attracted to sense objects for enjoyment, the mind becomes agitated. As a result of continually thinking of sense objects, one’s real consciousness almost becomes lost, like the water in a lake that is gradually sucked up by the big grass straws on its bank.

verses.sanskrit: भ्रश्यत्यनुस्मृतिश्चित्तं ज्ञानभ्रंश: स्मृतिक्षये ।तद्रोधं कवय: प्राहुरात्मापह्नवमात्मन: ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhraśyaty anusmṛtiś cittaṁjñāna-bhraṁśaḥ smṛti-kṣayetad-rodhaṁ kavayaḥ prāhurātmāpahnavam ātmanaḥ
verses.text: When one deviates from his original consciousness, he loses the capacity to remember his previous position or recognize his present one. When remembrance is lost, all knowledge acquired is based on a false foundation. When this occurs, learned scholars consider that the soul is lost.

verses.sanskrit: नात: परतरो लोके पुंस: स्वार्थव्यतिक्रम: ।यदध्यन्यस्य प्रेयस्त्वमात्मन: स्वव्यतिक्रमात् ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: nātaḥ parataro lokepuṁsaḥ svārtha-vyatikramaḥyad-adhy anyasya preyastvamātmanaḥ sva-vyatikramāt
verses.text: There is no stronger obstruction to one’s self-interest than thinking other subject matters to be more pleasing than one’s self-realization.

verses.sanskrit: अर्थेन्द्रियार्थाभिध्यानं सर्वार्थापह्नवो नृणाम् ।भ्रंशितो ज्ञानविज्ञानाद्येनाविशति मुख्यताम् ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: arthendriyārthābhidhyānaṁsarvārthāpahnavo nṛṇāmbhraṁśito jñāna-vijñānādyenāviśati mukhyatām
verses.text: For human society, constantly thinking of how to earn money and apply it for sense gratification brings about the destruction of everyone’s interests. When one becomes devoid of knowledge and devotional service, he enters into species of life like those of trees and stones.

verses.sanskrit: न कुर्यात्कर्हिचित्सङ्गं तमस्तीव्रं तितीरिषु: ।धर्मार्थकाममोक्षाणां यदत्यन्तविघातकम् ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: na kuryāt karhicit saṅgaṁtamas tīvraṁ titīriṣuḥdharmārtha-kāma-mokṣāṇāṁyad atyanta-vighātakam
verses.text: Those who strongly desire to cross the ocean of nescience must not associate with the modes of ignorance, for hedonistic activities are the greatest obstructions to realization of religious principles, economic development, regulated sense gratification and, at last, liberation.

verses.sanskrit: तत्रापि मोक्ष एवार्थ आत्यन्तिकतयेष्यते ।त्रैवर्ग्योऽर्थो यतो नित्यं कृतान्तभयसंयुत: ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatrāpi mokṣa evārthaātyantikatayeṣyatetraivargyo ’rtho yato nityaṁkṛtānta-bhaya-saṁyutaḥ
verses.text: Out of the four principles — namely religion, economic development, sense gratification and liberation — liberation has to be taken very seriously. The other three are subject to destruction by the stringent law of nature — death.

verses.sanskrit: परेऽवरे च ये भावा गुणव्यतिकरादनु ।न तेषां विद्यते क्षेममीशविध्वंसिताशिषाम् ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: pare ’vare ca ye bhāvāguṇa-vyatikarād anuna teṣāṁ vidyate kṣemamīśa-vidhvaṁsitāśiṣām
verses.text: We accept as blessings different states of higher life, distinguishing them from lower states of life, but we should know that such distinctions exist only in relation to the interchange of the modes of material nature. Actually these states of life have no permanent existence, for all of them will be destroyed by the supreme controller.

verses.sanskrit: तत्त्वं नरेन्द्र जगतामथ तस्थूषां चदेहेन्द्रियासुधिषणात्मभिरावृतानाम् ।य: क्षेत्रवित्तपतया हृदि विश्वगावि:प्रत्यक् चकास्ति भगवांस्तमवेहि सोऽस्मि ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tat tvaṁ narendra jagatām atha tasthūṣāṁ cadehendriyāsu-dhiṣaṇātmabhir āvṛtānāmyaḥ kṣetravit-tapatayā hṛdi viśvag āviḥpratyak cakāsti bhagavāṁs tam avehi so ’smi
verses.text: Sanat-kumāra advised the King: Therefore, my dear King Pṛthu, try to understand the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who is living within everyone’s heart along with the individual soul, in each and every body, either moving or not moving. The individual souls are fully covered by the gross material body and subtle body made of the life air and intelligence.

verses.sanskrit: यस्मिन्निदं सदसदात्मतया विभातिमाया विवेकविधुति स्रजि वाहिबुद्धि: ।तं नित्यमुक्तपरिशुद्धविशुद्धतत्त्वंप्रत्यूढकर्मकलिलप्रकृतिं प्रपद्ये ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: yasminn idaṁ sad-asad-ātmatayā vibhātimāyā viveka-vidhuti sraji vāhi-buddhiḥtaṁ nitya-mukta-pariśuddha-viśuddha-tattvaṁpratyūḍha-karma-kalila-prakṛtiṁ prapadye
verses.text: The Supreme Personality of Godhead manifests Himself as one with the cause and effect within this body, but one who has transcended the illusory energy by deliberate consideration, which clears the misconception of a snake for a rope, can understand that the Paramātmā is eternally transcendental to the material creation and situated in pure internal energy. Thus the Lord is transcendental to all material contamination. Unto Him only must one surrender.

verses.sanskrit: यत्पादपङ्कजपलाशविलासभक्त्याकर्माशयं ग्रथितमुद्ग्रथयन्ति सन्त: ।तद्वन्न रिक्तमतयो यतयोऽपि रुद्धस्रोतोगणास्तमरणं भज वासुदेवम् ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: yat-pāda-paṅkaja-palāśa-vilāsa-bhaktyākarmāśayaṁ grathitam udgrathayanti santaḥtadvan na rikta-matayo yatayo ’pi ruddha-sroto-gaṇās tam araṇaṁ bhaja vāsudevam
verses.text: The devotees, who are always engaged in the service of the toes of the lotus feet of the Lord, can very easily overcome hard-knotted desires for fruitive activities. Because this is very difficult, the nondevotees — the jñānīs and yogīs — although trying to stop the waves of sense gratification, cannot do so. Therefore you are advised to engage in the devotional service of Kṛṣṇa, the son of Vasudeva.

verses.sanskrit: कृच्छ्रो महानिह भवार्णवमप्लवेशांषड्‌वर्गनक्रमसुखेन तितीर्षन्ति ।तत्त्वं हरेर्भगवतो भजनीयमङ्‌घ्रिंकृत्वोडुपं व्यसनमुत्तर दुस्तरार्णम् ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: kṛcchro mahān iha bhavārṇavam aplaveśāṁṣaḍ-varga-nakram asukhena titīrṣantitat tvaṁ harer bhagavato bhajanīyam aṅghriṁkṛtvoḍupaṁ vyasanam uttara dustarārṇam
verses.text: The ocean of nescience is very difficult to cross because it is infested with many dangerous sharks. Although those who are nondevotees undergo severe austerities and penances to cross that ocean, we recommend that you simply take shelter of the lotus feet of the Lord, which are like boats for crossing the ocean. Although the ocean is difficult to cross, by taking shelter of His lotus feet you will overcome all dangers.

verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचस एवं ब्रह्मपुत्रेण कुमारेणात्ममेधसा ।दर्शितात्मगति: सम्यक्प्रशस्योवाच तं नृप: ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācasa evaṁ brahma-putreṇakumāreṇātma-medhasādarśitātma-gatiḥ samyakpraśasyovāca taṁ nṛpaḥ
verses.text: The great sage Maitreya continued: Being thus enlightened in complete spiritual knowledge by the son of Brahmā — one of the Kumāras, who was complete in spiritual knowledge — the King worshiped them in the following words.

verses.sanskrit: राजोवाचकृतो मेऽनुग्रह: पूर्वं हरिणार्तानुकम्पिना ।तमापादयितुं ब्रह्मन् भगवन् यूयमागता: ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: rājovācakṛto me ’nugrahaḥ pūrvaṁhariṇārtānukampinātam āpādayituṁ brahmanbhagavan yūyam āgatāḥ
verses.text: The King said: O brāhmaṇa, O powerful one, formerly Lord Viṣṇu showed me His causeless mercy, indicating that you would come to my house, and to confirm that blessing, you have all come.

verses.sanskrit: निष्पादितश्च कार्त्स्‍न्येन भगवद्‌भिर्घृणालुभि: ।साधूच्छिष्टं हि मे सर्वमात्मना सह किं ददे ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: niṣpāditaś ca kārtsnyenabhagavadbhir ghṛṇālubhiḥsādhūcchiṣṭaṁ hi me sarvamātmanā saha kiṁ dade
verses.text: My dear brāhmaṇa, you have carried out the order thoroughly because you are also as compassionate as the Lord. It is my duty, therefore, to offer you something, but all I possess are but remnants of food taken by great saintly persons. What shall I give?

verses.sanskrit: प्राणा दारा: सुता ब्रह्मन् गृहाश्च सपरिच्छदा: ।राज्यं बलं मही कोश इति सर्वं निवेदितम् ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: prāṇā dārāḥ sutā brahmangṛhāś ca sa-paricchadāḥrājyaṁ balaṁ mahī kośaiti sarvaṁ niveditam
verses.text: The King continued: Therefore, my dear brāhmaṇas, my life, wife, children, home, furniture and household paraphernalia, my kingdom, strength, land and especially my treasury are all offered unto you.

verses.sanskrit: सैनापत्यं च राज्यं च दण्डनेतृत्वमेव च ।सर्व लोकाधिपत्यं च वेदशास्त्रविदर्हति ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: sainā-patyaṁ ca rājyaṁ cadaṇḍa-netṛtvam eva casarva lokādhipatyaṁ caveda-śāstra-vid arhati
verses.text: Since only a person who is completely educated according to the principles of Vedic knowledge deserves to be commander-in-chief, ruler of the state, the first to chastise or the proprietor of the whole planet, Pṛthu Mahārāja offered everything to the Kumāras.

verses.sanskrit: स्वमेव ब्राह्मणो भुङ्क्ते स्वं वस्ते स्वं ददाति च ।तस्यैवानुग्रहेणान्नं भुञ्जते क्षत्रियादय: ॥ ४६ ॥
verses.transliteration: svam eva brāhmaṇo bhuṅktesvaṁ vaste svaṁ dadāti catasyaivānugraheṇānnaṁbhuñjate kṣatriyādayaḥ
verses.text: The kṣatriyas, vaiśyas and śūdras eat their food by virtue of the brāhmaṇas’ mercy. It is the brāhmaṇas who enjoy their own property, clothe themselves with their own property and give charity with their own property.

verses.sanskrit: यैरीद‍ृशी भगवतो गतिरात्मवादएकान्ततो निगमिभि: प्रतिपादिता न: ।तुष्यन्‍त्वदभ्रकरुणा: स्वकृतेन नित्यंको नाम तत्प्रतिकरोति विनोदपात्रम् ॥ ४७ ॥
verses.transliteration: yair īdṛśī bhagavato gatir ātma-vādaekāntato nigamibhiḥ pratipāditā naḥtuṣyantv adabhra-karuṇāḥ sva-kṛtena nityaṁko nāma tat pratikaroti vinoda-pātram
verses.text: Pṛthu Mahārāja continued: How can such persons, who have rendered unlimited service by explaining the path of self-realization in relation to the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and whose explanations are given for our enlightenment with complete conviction and Vedic evidence, be repaid except by folded palms containing water for their satisfaction? Such great personalities can be satisfied only by their own activities, which are distributed amongst human society out of their unlimited mercy.

verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचत आत्मयोगपतय आदिराजेन पूजिता: ।शीलं तदीयं शंसन्त: खेऽभवन्मिषतां नृणाम् ॥ ४८ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācata ātma-yoga-patayaādi-rājena pūjitāḥśīlaṁ tadīyaṁ śaṁsantaḥkhe ’bhavan miṣatāṁ nṛṇām
verses.text: The great sage Maitreya continued: Being thus worshiped by Mahārāja Pṛthu, the four Kumāras, who were masters of devotional service, became very pleased. Indeed, they appeared in the sky and praised the character of the King, and everyone observed them.

verses.sanskrit: वैन्यस्तु धुर्यो महतां संस्थित्याध्यात्मशिक्षया ।आप्तकाममिवात्मानं मेन आत्मन्यवस्थित: ॥ ४९ ॥
verses.transliteration: vainyas tu dhuryo mahatāṁsaṁsthityādhyātma-śikṣayāāpta-kāmam ivātmānaṁmena ātmany avasthitaḥ
verses.text: Amongst great personalities, Mahārāja Pṛthu was the chief by virtue of his fixed position in relation to spiritual enlightenment. He remained satisfied as one who has achieved all success in spiritual understanding.

verses.sanskrit: कर्माणि च यथाकालं यथादेशं यथाबलम् ।यथोचितं यथावित्तमकरोद्ब्रह्मसात्कृतम् ॥ ५० ॥
verses.transliteration: karmāṇi ca yathā-kālaṁyathā-deśaṁ yathā-balamyathocitaṁ yathā-vittamakarod brahma-sāt-kṛtam
verses.text: Being self-satisfied, Mahārāja Pṛthu executed his duties as perfectly as possible according to the time and his situation, strength and financial position. His only aim in all his activities was to satisfy the Absolute Truth. In this way, he duly acted.

verses.sanskrit: फलं ब्रह्मणि संन्यस्य निर्विषङ्ग: समाहित: ।कर्माध्यक्षं च मन्वान आत्मानं प्रकृते: परम् ॥ ५१ ॥
verses.transliteration: phalaṁ brahmaṇi sannyasyanirviṣaṅgaḥ samāhitaḥkarmādhyakṣaṁ ca manvānaātmānaṁ prakṛteḥ param
verses.text: Mahārāja Pṛthu completely dedicated himself to be an eternal servant of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, transcendental to material nature. Consequently all the fruits of his activities were dedicated to the Lord, and he always thought of himself as the servant of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who is the proprietor of everything.

verses.sanskrit: गृहेषु वर्तमानोऽपि स साम्राज्यश्रियान्वित: ।नासज्जतेन्द्रियार्थेषु निरहंमतिरर्कवत् ॥ ५२ ॥
verses.transliteration: gṛheṣu vartamāno ’pisa sāmrājya-śriyānvitaḥnāsajjatendriyārtheṣuniraham-matir arkavat
verses.text: Mahārāja Pṛthu, who was very opulent due to the prosperity of his entire empire, remained at home as a householder. Since he was never inclined to utilize his opulences for the gratification of his senses, he remained unattached, exactly like the sun, which is unaffected in all circumstances.

verses.sanskrit: एवमध्यात्मयोगेन कर्माण्यनुसमाचरन् ।पुत्रानुत्पादयामास पञ्चार्चिष्यात्मसम्मतान् ॥ ५३ ॥
verses.transliteration: evam adhyātma-yogenakarmāṇy anusamācaranputrān utpādayām āsapañcārciṣy ātma-sammatān
verses.text: Being situated in the liberated position of devotional service, Pṛthu Mahārāja not only performed all fruitive activities but also begot five sons by his wife, Arci. Indeed, all his sons were begotten according to his own desire.

verses.sanskrit: विजिताश्वं धूम्रकेशं हर्यक्षं द्रविणंवृकम् ।सर्वेषां लोकपालानां दधारैक: पृथुर्गुणान् ॥ ५४ ॥
verses.transliteration: vijitāśvaṁ dhūmrakeśaṁharyakṣaṁ draviṇaṁ vṛkamsarveṣāṁ loka-pālānāṁdadhāraikaḥ pṛthur guṇān
verses.text: After begetting five sons, named Vijitāśva, Dhūmrakeśa, Haryakṣa, Draviṇa and Vṛka, Pṛthu Mahārāja continued to rule the planet. He accepted all the qualities of the deities who governed all other planets.

verses.sanskrit: गोपीथाय जगत्सृष्टे: काले स्वेस्वेऽच्युतात्मक: ।मनोवाग्वृत्तिभि: सौम्यैर्गुणै: संरञ्जयन्प्रजा: ॥ ५५ ॥
verses.transliteration: gopīthāya jagat-sṛṣṭeḥkāle sve sve ’cyutātmakaḥmano-vāg-vṛttibhiḥ saumyairguṇaiḥ saṁrañjayan prajāḥ
verses.text: Since Mahārāja Pṛthu was a perfect devotee of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, he wanted to protect the Lord’s creation by pleasing the various citizens according to their various desires. Therefore Pṛthu Mahārāja used to please them in all respects by his words, mentality, works and gentle behavior.

verses.sanskrit: राजेत्यधान्नामधेयं सोमराज इवापर: ।सूर्यवद्विसृजन् गृह्णन् प्रतपंश्च भुवो वसु ॥ ५६ ॥
verses.transliteration: rājety adhān nāmadheyaṁsoma-rāja ivāparaḥsūryavad visṛjan gṛhṇanpratapaṁś ca bhuvo vasu
verses.text: Mahārāja Pṛthu became as celebrated a king as Soma-rāja, the king of the moon. He was also powerful and exacting, just like the sun-god, who distributes heat and light and at the same time exacts all the planetary waters.

verses.sanskrit: दुर्धर्षस्तेजसेवाग्निर्महेन्द्र इव दुर्जय: ।तितिक्षया धरित्रीव द्यौरिवाभीष्टदो नृणाम् ॥ ५७ ॥
verses.transliteration: durdharṣas tejasevāgnirmahendra iva durjayaḥtitikṣayā dharitrīvadyaur ivābhīṣṭa-do nṛṇām
verses.text: Mahārāja Pṛthu was so strong and powerful that no one could disobey his orders, any more than one could conquer fire itself. He was so strong that he was compared to Indra, the King of heaven, whose power is insuperable. On the other hand, Mahārāja Pṛthu was also as tolerant as the earth, and in fulfilling various desires of human society, he was like heaven itself.

verses.sanskrit: वर्षति स्म यथाकामं पर्जन्य इव तर्पयन् ।समुद्र इव दुर्बोध: सत्त्वेनाचलराडिव ॥ ५८ ॥
verses.transliteration: varṣati sma yathā-kāmaṁparjanya iva tarpayansamudra iva durbodhaḥsattvenācala-rāḍ iva
verses.text: Just as rainfall satisfies everyone’s desires, Mahārāja Pṛthu used to satisfy everyone. He was like the sea in that no one could understand his depths, and he was like Meru, the king of hills, in the fixity of his purpose.

verses.sanskrit: धर्मराडिव शिक्षायामाश्चर्ये हिमवानिव ।कुवेर इव कोशाढ्यो गुप्तार्थो वरुणो यथा ॥ ५९ ॥
verses.transliteration: dharma-rāḍ iva śikṣāyāmāścarye himavān ivakuvera iva kośāḍhyoguptārtho varuṇo yathā
verses.text: Mahārāja Pṛthu’s intelligence and education were exactly like that of Yamarāja, the superintendent of death. His opulence was comparable to the Himālaya Mountains, where all valuable jewels and metals are stocked. He possessed great riches like Kuvera, the treasurer of the heavenly planets, and no one could reveal his secrets, for they were like the demigod Varuṇa’s.

verses.sanskrit: मातरिश्वेव सर्वात्मा बलेन महसौजसा ।अविषह्यतया देवो भगवान् भूतराडिव ॥ ६० ॥
verses.transliteration: mātariśveva sarvātmābalena mahasaujasāaviṣahyatayā devobhagavān bhūta-rāḍ iva
verses.text: In his bodily strength and in the strength of his senses, Mahārāja Pṛthu was as strong as the wind, which can go anywhere and everywhere. As far as his intolerance was concerned, he was just like the all-powerful Rudra expansion of Lord Śiva, or Sadāśiva.

verses.sanskrit: कन्दर्प इव सौन्दर्ये मनस्वी मृगराडिव ।वात्सल्ये मनुवन्नृणां प्रभुत्वे भगवानज: ॥ ६१ ॥
verses.transliteration: kandarpa iva saundaryemanasvī mṛga-rāḍ ivavātsalye manuvan nṛṇāṁprabhutve bhagavān ajaḥ
verses.text: In his bodily beauty he was just like Cupid, and in his thoughtfulness he was like a lion. In his affection he was just like Svāyambhuva Manu, and in his ability to control he was like Lord Brahmā.

verses.sanskrit: बृहस्पतिर्ब्रह्मवादे आत्मवत्त्वे स्वयं हरि: ।भक्त्या गोगुरुविप्रेषु विष्वक्सेनानुवर्तिषु ।ह्रिया प्रश्रयशीलाभ्यामात्मतुल्य: परोद्यमे ॥ ६२ ॥
verses.transliteration: bṛhaspatir brahma-vādeātmavattve svayaṁ hariḥbhaktyā go-guru-vipreṣuviṣvaksenānuvartiṣuhriyā praśraya-śīlābhyāmātma-tulyaḥ parodyame
verses.text: In his personal behavior, Pṛthu Mahārāja exhibited all good qualities, and in spiritual knowledge he was exactly like Bṛhaspati. In self-control he was like the Supreme Personality of Godhead Himself. As far as his devotional service was concerned, he was a great follower of devotees who were attached to cow protection and the rendering of all service to the spiritual master and the brāhmaṇas. He was perfect in his shyness and in his gentle behavior, and when he engaged in some philanthropic activity, he worked as if he were working for his own personal self.

verses.sanskrit: कीर्त्योर्ध्वगीतया पुम्भिस्त्रैलोक्ये तत्र तत्र ह ।प्रविष्ट: कर्णरन्ध्रेषु स्त्रीणां राम: सतामिव ॥ ६३ ॥
verses.transliteration: kīrtyordhva-gītayā pumbhistrailokye tatra tatra hapraviṣṭaḥ karṇa-randhreṣustrīṇāṁ rāmaḥ satām iva
verses.text: Throughout the whole universe — in the higher, lower and middle planetary systems — Pṛthu Mahārāja’s reputation was loudly declared, and all ladies and saintly persons heard his glories, which were as sweet as the glories of Lord Rāmacandra.
